disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sudden high fever and shivering starting about 12 hours ago
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It came on suddenly about 12 hours ago — a high temperature and shivering, and it's been getting worse since.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Fast, laboured breathing with visible use of neck and chest muscles
trigger_question_examples: Is he working hard to breathe?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He's breathing really fast and hard — you can see him using his neck and chest muscles, and we counted about 28 breaths a minute.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Wet cough producing yellow phlegm that he is too weak to clear
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He's got a wet, chesty cough with yellow phlegm, but he's too weak to cough it up properly — his cough is always a bit weak because of his low muscle tone.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Marked drop from baseline — usually chatty and independent, now too weak to get out of bed or drink and very withdrawn
trigger_question_examples: How is he in himself compared to normal?
Is he more drowsy or withdrawn than usual?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: This is what's really scaring us — he's normally chatty and gets around fine, but now he's too weak to get out of bed or drink and he's barely responding to us.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: Maybe he's just tired… but no, this really isn't like him at all.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Home oximeter reading around 92% on room air
trigger_question_examples: Do you have a home oxygen monitor? What does it read?
Have you checked his oxygen levels?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: We put the finger oximeter on and it's reading about 92%.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Known Down syndrome with a heart defect surgically repaired in childhood, and baseline low muscle tone with a weak cough
trigger_question_examples: Does he have any ongoing medical conditions?
Any heart or breathing problems normally?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He has Down syndrome. He had a hole in his heart fixed with an operation when he was little — there's a scar on his chest. His muscle tone is low so his cough is always a bit weak.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Not eating or drinking since this began
trigger_question_examples: Is he managing to drink anything?
Has he eaten or had fluids today?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, he won't take any fluids or food — he's too weak and drowsy.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: He cannot reliably describe his own symptoms today; the support worker is giving the history
trigger_question_examples: Is he able to tell me how he feels?
Who is with him and can help describe things?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He usually talks and tells us how he feels, but right now he's too drowsy to say much, so I'm describing it for him as his support worker.
